title: Write hs5t-ring-buffer unit + proptest tests (RED phase)
description: 6 test cases: round-trip, is_full/write-err, wrap-around, two-iovec path, read_finish/read_release distinction, proptest random sequences. All tests must FAIL before implementation.

title: Implement hs5t-ring-buffer (GREEN phase)
description: Implement RingBuffer struct with rp/wp/rda_size/use_size/max_size/data fields. Implement reading/read_finish/read_release/writing/write_finish methods. Minimum code to pass all tests.

title: Wire differential test: C vs Rust ring buffer (10k proptest cases)
description: Enable build.rs to compile src/misc/hev-ring-buffer.c via cc crate. Write ring_buffer_diff.rs with extern C declarations. Generate 10k proptest sequences; assert byte-exact match and state match at every step.

title: Verify ASAN clean on hs5t-ring-buffer (nightly)
description: Run: RUSTFLAGS='-Z sanitizer=address' cargo +nightly test -p hs5t-ring-buffer. Fix any issues found. Document nightly toolchain requirement if not installed.

title: Write hs5t-logger unit tests (RED phase)
description: 4 test cases: level filter (debug suppressed at Info), file output format [timestamp] [L] msg\n, multiple log levels (Debug/Info/Warn/Error), exec equivalent (Command::new(script) exits 0).

title: Implement hs5t-logger (GREEN phase)
description: Use tracing + tracing-subscriber for leveled logging with EnvFilter. Use std::process::Command for exec equivalent. Minimum code to pass all logger tests.

title: Final: clippy -D warnings clean, commit, write LOOP_COMPLETE
description: Run cargo clippy --workspace -D warnings. Fix all warnings. Commit all changes with Conventional Commits. Emit LOOP_COMPLETE signal.

title: Write hs5t-config unit tests (RED phase)
description: 10 test cases: (1) parse conf/main.yml all fields match, (2) missing socks5.port -> Err, (3) missing socks5.address -> Err, (4) username+no password -> Err, (5) password+no username -> Err, (6) all defaults when optional sections absent, (7) mapdns defaults, (8) misc.task_stack_size default=86016, (9) misc.tcp_buffer_size default=65536, (10) misc.udp_recv_buffer_size default=524288. All tests must FAIL before implementation.

title: Implement hs5t-config (GREEN phase)
description: Implement Config struct with serde + serde_yaml: TunnelConfig, Socks5Config (address+port required), MapdnsConfig (optional), MiscConfig (all optional with defaults). Validate: socks5.address required, socks5.port required, username/password both-or-neither. derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize). Must be Send+Sync. All 10 unit tests must PASS.

title: Write config proptest property-based tests
description: In rust/tests/differential/ or hs5t-config tests: (1) generate random valid YAML configs (socks5.address+port always present), verify parse() succeeds and roundtrips; (2) generate configs with missing socks5.address or port, verify parse() fails; (3) generate random auth combos, verify both-or-neither rule.

title: Write differential test: C vs Rust config parsing
description: In rust/tests/differential/config_diff.rs: write a thin C wrapper (no libyaml — parse YAML manually or use a simplified stub) exposing test_config_from_str() returning a flat CConfigSnapshot struct. OR: if libyaml submodule not available, document as deferred and implement 12-variant YAML test that validates Rust alone. Test 12 YAML variants: all fields, defaults only, missing required, auth combos. Assert every field matches expected values.

title: Write fuzz target for config parser
description: Create fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_config.rs: take arbitrary bytes as YAML input, call hs5t_config::Config::from_str(), verify no panic (Err is acceptable, panic is not). Run with cargo fuzz for 1M iterations. Add to Cargo.toml under [workspace] if needed.

title: Final: clippy -D warnings clean, commit, LOOP_COMPLETE
description: Run: cargo clippy --workspace -D warnings. Fix all warnings. Run cargo test --workspace to confirm all tests pass. Commit all hs5t-config changes with Conventional Commits. Emit LOOP_COMPLETE signal by writing to the designated file.

title: Define TunnelError + TunnelDriver trait in hs5t-tunnel lib.rs
description: Create TunnelError enum and TunnelDriver trait as specified. This is the trait contract that all platform impls must satisfy.

title: Implement LinuxTunnel in hs5t-tunnel/src/linux.rs
description: Full Linux TUN impl: open /dev/net/tun, TUNSETIFF, SIOCSIFMTU, SIOCSIFADDR, SIOCSIFNETMASK, SIOCSIFFLAGS. Use nix crate. OwnedFd owns the fd. All unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments.

title: Write integration tests for LinuxTunnel (CAP_NET_ADMIN, #[ignore])
description: 5 tests: fd valid, set_mtu(1500), set_ipv4(198.18.0.1/32), set_up/set_down, drop closes fd via fcntl. All marked #[ignore]. Run with: sudo cargo test -p hs5t-tunnel -- --include-ignored

title: Implement macOS, FreeBSD, NetBSD, Windows stubs in hs5t-tunnel
description: macOS: SYSPROTO_CONTROL + UTUN_CONTROL_NAME socket approach (compile-only). FreeBSD/NetBSD/Windows: return TunnelError::NotSupported. All platform-gated via cfg.

title: Final: run Linux tests + unsafe grep check + clippy + commit + LOOP_COMPLETE
description: 1. sudo cargo test -p hs5t-tunnel -- --include-ignored (all pass). 2. CI grep check: zero unsafe blocks without SAFETY comment. 3. cargo clippy -D warnings. 4. cargo check --target x86_64-apple-darwin -p hs5t-tunnel. 5. Commit. 6. Write LOOP_COMPLETE.

title: Write RED unit tests for DnsCache (9 test cases)
description: Write all 9 failing tests first: IP allocation, LRU eviction, handle() valid/invalid packets, reverse lookup, DNS response flags. Tests in rust/crates/hs5t-dns-cache/src/lib.rs

title: Implement DnsCache: struct + find() + lookup() (GREEN)
description: Implement DnsCache with LruCache<String,u32> + HashMap<u32,String>. find() assigns net|idx, keeps reverse map in sync on eviction. lookup() returns name by ip.

title: Implement DnsCache::handle() - DNS wire format parser (GREEN)
description: Parse DNS request: header(12B), questions(name labels+QTYPE+QCLASS), build A-record answers. Mutate name labels in-place (length->dot). Flags: QR=1,AA=1,RA=(RD>>1). Never panic, return Err on malformed.

title: Write proptest property-based tests for DnsCache
description: Random find()+handle()+lookup() sequences. Invariant: LRU always evicts LRU name. Invariant: reverse map always consistent with forward map. Add proptest to dev-dependencies.

title: Write standalone C shim for DNS differential test
description: hev-task-system submodule not initialized. Write self-contained C shim (dns_shim.c) that reimplements hev_mapped_dns_handle() without submodule deps. Use cc crate to compile. Place in rust/tests/differential/src/dns_shim.c

title: Write 500-case DNS differential test (Rust vs C shim)
description: In tests/differential/src/dns_diff.rs: generate 500 random valid A-query packets, run both C shim and Rust handle(), assert byte-exact response match and IP mapping match.

title: Write fuzz target for DnsCache::handle()
description: In rust/fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dns_handle.rs: feed arbitrary bytes, assert no panic (only Ok/Err). Also assert: if byte input would make C return -1, Rust returns Err.

title: Final: ASAN clean + clippy -D warnings + commit + LOOP_COMPLETE
description: Run cargo test --workspace, clippy -D warnings, check no unsafe outside differential shim, commit, write LOOP_COMPLETE.
